info.description: The Endpoint Catalog API provides a FHIR R4 interface for managing and querying Endpoint and HealthcareService resources. It enables connecting parties to discover service endpoints by organisation, service identifier, connection type and payload type.
info.title: Endpoint Catalog API

get.summary: Get the Endpoint Catalogue API capability statement
get.description:
Returns the CapabilityStatement for the Endpoint Catalogue API.

This describes the resources, interactions, and search parameters supported by this API. Consumers can use this to programmatically discover what the API supports.

No NHSD-Target-Identifier header is required.

200.description: The CapabilityStatement for the Endpoint Catalogue API

implementation.description: Endpoint Catalogue API - FHIR R4 service discovery

X-Correlation-Id.description: The X-Correlation-Id from the request header, if supplied, mirrored back.

X-Request-Id.description: The X-Request-Id from the request header, if supplied, mirrored back.

get.summary: Get the HealthcareService for an identifier
get.description:
Get the HealthcareService for an identifier.

**Note:** `_include=HealthcareService:endpoint` is not supported on this endpoint. The `GET /Endpoint` search provides visibility filtering (status, period, HealthcareService.active) and supports filtering by `connectionType` and `payloadType`, which cannot be applied through `_include`.

To retrieve Endpoints for a HealthcareService, use:
```
GET /Endpoint?_has:HealthcareService:endpoint:_id={id}
```
Read operation for a Specific HealthcareService

put.description: Creates a new current version for an existing resource [(update)](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#update) or creates an initial version if no resource already exists for the given id [(new)](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#upsert).

get.summary: Search for Endpoints by organisation, HealthcareService, connection type and payload type
get.description:
Search for Endpoint resources. Results can be filtered by organisation, HealthcareService association, connection type, and payload type.

The Endpoint.address will be omitted from results where Endpoint.header is set to private, unless the requestor is the owner of that Endpoint.

### Query parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| _has | string | Filter by associated HealthcareService. Use _has:HealthcareService.Identifier=<system>\|<value> or _has:HealthcareService.Id=<uuid> |
| identifier | string | Filter by the Endpoint's own identifier (not the resource id). URL-encode the value. |
| connection-type | string | Filter by technical protocol, e.g. hl7-fhir-rest. Matches against Endpoint.connectionType.coding.code. |
| payload-type | string | Filter by message standard, e.g. bars. Matches against Endpoint.payloadType.coding.code. |

get.summary: Get the Endpoint template based on an Organization, Endpoint, ConnectionType and PayloadType identifiers
get.description: Get the Endpoint template for an identifier. Its address will be omitted if the Endpoint.header is set to private, unless the requestor is the owner.

post.description: Create a single Endpoint template resource.

get.description: Read operation for a specific Endpoint

put.description:
Creates a new current version for an existing resource ([update](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#update)) or creates an initial version if no resource already exists for the given id ([new](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#upsert)).

Only the owner of an Endpoint can modify it.

delete.description:
Delete a Specific Endpoint.

Only the owner of an Endpoint can delete it.

put.description:
Creates a new current version for an existing resource ([update](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#update)) or creates an initial version if no resource already exists for the given id ([new](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#upsert)).

Only the owner of an Endpoint can modify its template.

delete.description:
Delete a Specific Endpoint template.

Only the owner of an Endpoint can delete its template.

post.summary: Create a priority-ordered Endpoint list for a HealthcareService
post.description:
Create a FHIR R4 [List](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/list.html) resource representing the priority-ordered set of Endpoints for a HealthcareService.

Neither `Endpoint` nor `HealthcareService` carry a preferred order — the `List` resource is the mechanism for expressing communication preference.

- `List.subject` must reference a `HealthcareService` resource
- Each `List.entry[].item` must reference an `Endpoint` resource
- Priority is determined by array position — index 0 is the highest priority
- `List.orderedBy` must be set to `priority`
- Only one `current` list should exist per HealthcareService

get.description:
Search for `List` resources. Use `subject` to find the priority-ordered Endpoint list for a specific HealthcareService.

Use `_include=List:item` to return the referenced `Endpoint` resources inline in the same Bundle, avoiding a second request.

### Important: No connectionType or payloadType filtering on this endpoint

`GET /List` does **not** support filtering by `connectionType` or `payloadType`. This is because:

1. The `List` resource is a pure ordering mechanism — it holds references to Endpoints by ID, with no knowledge of what type each Endpoint is.
2. `connectionType` and `payloadType` are properties of the Endpoint (resolved from its parent Template at read time). They are not properties of the List or its entries.
3. FHIR `_include` has no filter mechanism — when `_include=List:item` is used, all referenced Endpoints are returned regardless of their type.

If you need **both** priority ordering **and** type filtering, use the two-step pattern:

1. `GET /List?subject=HealthcareService/{id}&status=current` — retrieve the priority order
2. `GET /Endpoint?_has:HealthcareService:endpoint:_id={id}&ConnectionType={type}&PayloadType={type}` — retrieve type-filtered Endpoints
3. Client-side: apply the List order to the filtered results (iterate `List.entry[]`, skip entries not in the Step 2 response, preserve relative order of the rest)

### No visibility filtering applied

No visibility filtering (status, period, or HealthcareService.active) is applied to Endpoints returned via `_include` on this endpoint. `/List` is intended for Endpoint administration and priority management, not consumer routing — administrators need to see all Endpoints regardless of their operational state.

### Query parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `subject` | string | Filter by HealthcareService reference, e.g. `HealthcareService/{id}` |
| `status` | string | Filter by list status: `current`, `retired`, `entered-in-error` |
| `_include` | string | Include referenced Endpoint resources. Use `List:item` |

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Filter lists by HealthcareService reference. Use the relative reference format `HealthcareService/{id}`.

parameters.name: _include
parameters.description: Include referenced Endpoint resources in the response Bundle. Note: No connectionType, payloadType, or visibility filtering is applied to the included Endpoints. The List resource holds Endpoint references by ID only — it has no knowledge of Endpoint types. All referenced Endpoints are returned regardless of their connectionType, payloadType, status, or period. This is intentional: /List is an administration resource for managing priority ordering, not a consumer routing endpoint. Consumers needing type-filtered, ordered Endpoints should use the two-step pattern described in the operation description above.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The logical id of the List resource.

get.description: Read a specific FHIR R4 List resource by its logical id.

put.description: Creates a new current version for an existing List resource ([update](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#update)) or creates an initial version if no resource already exists for the given id ([new](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/http.html#upsert)).

delete.description: Delete a specific FHIR R4 List resource by its logical id.

CorrelationId_HParam.name: X-Correlation-Id
CorrelationId_HParam.description: The X-Correlation-Id for the request header, when supplied, mirrored back by the receiver.

AcceptEPC_HParam.name: Accept
AcceptEPC_HParam.description: The Accept header must specify the type of content the requester can process.

ConnectionType_QParam.name: ConnectionType

PayloadType_QParam.name: PayloadType

HealthcareServiceIdentifier_QParam.name: HealthcareService.identifier
HealthcareServiceIdentifier_QParam.description:
The identifier of the HealthcareService.
This needs to be Url Encoded as its passed as a query parameter and part of the url.

HCProvidedBy_QParam.name: HealthcareService.providedBy
HCProvidedBy_QParam.description:
The identifier of the organization that is managing the endpoint(s).
This needs to be Url Encoded as its passed as a query parameter and part of the url.

EndpointIdentifier_QParam.name: Endpoint.identifier
EndpointIdentifier_QParam.description:
The identifier of the *type* of Endpoint, usually the identifier set by the organisation/supplier that created the Endpoint. 
It is important to note that this is **not** the same as **Endpoint.id**, which is the unique identifier for the Endpoint resource. The Endpoint.identifier can be shared across multiple Endpoint resources.
This needs to be Url Encoded as its passed as a query parameter and part of the url.

EndpointHas_Qparam.name: _has:HealthcareService:endpoint:_id
EndpointHas_Qparam.description:
Reverse chained search — return Endpoints that are referenced by a HealthcareService with the given resource id.

Follows the FHIR R4 _has reverse chaining syntax (see [FHIR R4 §3.1.1.4.16](https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/search.html#has)):

``
GET [base]/Endpoint?_has:HealthcareService:endpoint:_id={serviceId}
`

| Segment | Meaning |
|---------|----------|
| HealthcareService | The resource type that refers back to Endpoint |
| endpoint | The search parameter on HealthcareService that holds the Endpoint reference |
| _id | Filter the HealthcareService by its resource id |
| {serviceId} | UUID of the HealthcareService whose referenced Endpoints you want |

**Example:**
`
GET /Endpoint?_has:HealthcareService:endpoint:_id=9f2c6f12-1a6d-4d9c-a111-123456789abc
``

EndpointIdentifier_PParam.name: id
EndpointIdentifier_PParam.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint.

RequestingOrganisationODS_HParam.name: NHSD-End-User-Organisation-ODS
RequestingOrganisationODS_HParam.description: The ODS code of the end-user organisation making the request.

ServiceId_QParam.name: _id
ServiceId_QParam.description:
Filter by the HealthcareService resource id (UUID).

Returns the specific HealthcareService matching the given id.

To retrieve the Endpoints associated with a HealthcareService, use GET /Endpoint with the _has:HealthcareService:endpoint:_id parameter.

Example:
GET /HealthcareService?_id=9f2c6f12-1a6d-4d9c-a111-123456789abc

NHSD-End-User-Organisation-ODS.name: NHSD-End-User-Organisation-ODS
NHSD-End-User-Organisation-ODS.description: The ODS code of the organisation making the request. Must match the organisation claim in the bearer token. Used for authorisation (ownership check) and audit.

4XX.description:
Client error. The request could not be processed due to an issue with the request itself.

| HTTP status | Error code | Description |
| ----------- | ---------- | ----------- |
| 400 | BAD_REQUEST | The request is malformed or missing required fields |
| 401 | UNAUTHORIZED | Missing or invalid bearer token |
| 403 | FORBIDDEN | The authenticated caller is not authorised for this operation |
| 404 | NOT_FOUND | The requested resource does not exist |
| 409 | CONFLICT | A duplicate resource exists or a conflict was detected |
| 422 | UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY | The request body failed validation |
| 429 | TOO_MANY_REQUESTS | Rate limit exceeded |

issue.diagnostics: Attempts to Validate the message have failed.

X-Correlation-Id.description: The X-Correlation-Id from the request header, if supplied, mirrored back.

X-Request-Id.description: The X-Request-Id from the request header, if supplied, mirrored back.

5XX.description:
Server error. The request could not be processed due to an issue on the server.

| HTTP status | Error code | Description |
| ----------- | ---------- | ----------- |
| 500 | INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred |
| 502 | BAD_GATEWAY | The upstream service returned an invalid response |
| 503 | SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE | The service is temporarily unavailable |
| 504 | GATEWAY_TIMEOUT | The upstream service did not respond in time |

issue.diagnostics: Too many requests have been made by this source in a given amount of time: <further diagnostics information, error message/error text>

description.example: Endpoint Catalogue API — FHIR R4 service discovery

FhirList.description: A FHIR R4 List resource representing a priority-ordered collection of Endpoint resources for a specific HealthcareService. The `subject` references the owning HealthcareService. Each `entry[].item` references an Endpoint resource. Priority is determined by array position — index 0 is the highest priority. `orderedBy` must be set to `priority`.

status.description: Indicates the current state of this list.

mode.description: How this list was prepared. Use `working` for a list maintained over time.

title.description: A human-readable label for the list.

code.description: Classifies the purpose of the list.

subject.description: The HealthcareService this list belongs to. All Endpoint entries in the list are associated with this service.

reference.description: Relative reference to the HealthcareService, e.g. `HealthcareService/{id}`.

display.description: Plain text name of the HealthcareService.

date.description: When the list was last changed.

orderedBy.description: Declares the ordering semantics. Must be `priority` to indicate entries are ordered by array position (index 0 = highest priority).

entry.description: The ordered list of Endpoint references. Array position determines priority — index 0 is the highest priority Endpoint. Consumers MUST use array position as the authoritative order.

deleted.description: True if this entry has been removed from the list.

date.description: When this entry was added to the list.

item.description: A reference to an Endpoint resource.

reference.description: Relative reference to an Endpoint resource, e.g. `Endpoint/{id}`.

display.description: Plain text name of the Endpoint.

OAuth_AppRestricted.description: Application-restricted access. Bearer token obtained via signed JWT authentication. Used for system-to-system operations (read and automated writes).

OAuth_CIS2.description: User-restricted access via NHS CIS2. Bearer token obtained via CIS2 authentication flow. Used for administrative operations where a human user is present.
